# Instructions for GitHub Copilot

1. When building code think about re-useability. If you find yourself writing the same code more than once, consider creating a function or component that can be reused.
2. When writing code, try to follow the existing code style and conventions used in the project. This includes things like naming conventions, indentation, and formatting.
3. When writing code, try to write clear and concise comments that explain the purpose of the code and how it works. This will help other developers understand your code and make it easier to maintain
4. Do not run security testing tools (e.g. CodeQL) on the codebase, as they may produce false positives and cause unnecessary noise in the development process. I'll specifically ask for security testing if I want it.
